certificate.moduleName: Juniper Networks EX, QFX and ACX Series
certificate.caveat: When installed, initialized and configured as specified in Section 11.1 of the Security Policy.
certificate.description: Junos® OS automates network operations with streamlined precision, furthers operational efficiency, and frees up valuable time and resources for top-line growth opportunities. Built for reliability, security, and flexibility, Junos OS runs many of the world’s most sophisticated network deployments, giving operators an advantage over those who run competing network operating systems.

sections.title: Tested and Vendor Affirmed Module Version and Identification

sensitiveSecurityParameters.name: Password authentication
sensitiveSecurityParameters.description: User and CO authentication via SSH or consol. Minimum of 10 ASCII character passwords.

extraColumns.Strength per Minute: Timed access mechanism allows max of 10 attempts / min. Probability of guessing: 10/(96^10) < 1/100,000.

sensitiveSecurityParameters.name: Signature authentication
sensitiveSecurityParameters.description: User/CO authentication via SSH
sensitiveSecurityParameters.strength: Strength of signature algorithm, minimum 112-bits. Probability of success for random attempt: 1/(2^112) < 1/1,000,000.

extraColumns.Strength per Minute: A rate of 1 CPU cycle per failed authentication for the Intel Xeon D-1518 processor (4 cores, 2.2 GHz) allows for the probability of success by brute- force attack: 60 x 4 x 2.2 x 10^9 x 1/(2^112) < 1/100,000.

sensitiveSecurityParameters.name: RAM
sensitiveSecurityParameters.description: Random Access Memory

sensitiveSecurityParameters.description: Shared DH value computed from the ephemeral DH key- pairs as part of SSH and used to derive session keys.

sensitiveSecurityParameters.description: SSH Private host key. 1st time SSH is configured, the keys are generated.

sensitiveSecurityParameters.description: SSH KAS private key

sensitiveSecurityParameters.description: SSH Session Encryption Keys

sensitiveSecurityParameters.description: Password used to authenticate the CO.

sensitiveSecurityParameters.description: Password used by CO to authenticate as 'root'.

sensitiveSecurityParameters.description: X.509 Certificate used to verify the validity of the Juniper Package CA

sensitiveSecurityParameters.name: Package- CA
sensitiveSecurityParameters.description: X.509 Certificate used to verify the validity the Juniper Image at software load and also at runtime for integrity.

services.name: Perform self-tests
services.description: On demand execution of all pre-operational and conditional algorithm self- tests

services.name: Critical Failure State
services.description: The cryptographic module ceases to perform cryptographic operations, inhibits all data output, and provides status of the error via syslog messages and console status output

services.name: Soft Error State
services.description: A non-critical self-test failure occurs, causing a failure of the triggering operation
services.roleAccess: PCT, firmware load test, continuous entropy health test failure

extraColumns.col3: The module processes the error, and resumes normal operation

selfTests.details: Checks that any file that is executed is registered in a manifest of executable files that comes with the firmware. Test verifies the integrity of the operational environment is being enforced by having the kernel attempt to run a specific executable file that does not contain a hash in the manifest file, verifying it cannot be executed.
selfTests.name: Critical functions test

On demand execution of all pre-operational and conditional algorithm self- tests
This command erases all data, including all configuration information, returning the module to its factory default state The system is then rebooted.
This command erases all keys and CSPS from storage. The forced power cycle also zeroizes SSPs in volatile memory.
Yes, CO via invocation of zeroize CLI command.
Zeroization of SSPs in RAM via invocation of local or remote reset service.
RAM is volatile and all data is lost when power is taken off. Zeroization is practically instantaneous.
Yes, both User and CO, via invocation of Local Reset or Remote Reset services.
Zeroization of SSPs in memory when no longer needed.
Use of explicit zeroization function destroys SSP information immediately by overwriting memory area with zeroes.
No. The operator cannot directly initiate this method.

Checks that any file that is executed is registered in a manifest of executable files that comes with the firmware. Test verifies the integrity of the operational environment is being enforced by having the kernel attempt to run a specific executable file that does not contain a hash in the manifest file, verifying it cannot be executed.
